title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Inflation and Child Plans: What Must You Do?
If you look at the past five years (2019-2024), the inflation rate has not fallen below 4%[1]. As a parent, planning for your child's future is one of the most important financial decisions to make. Inflation significantly impact this process. In this blog, we’ll explore the impact of inflation on child plans, the steps you can take to safeguard your investments, and practical strategies to ensure your child’s future is financially secure.
What is Inflation and Why Does it Matter?
Inflation is the rate at which the prices of goods and services rise over time, lowering the buying power of money[2]. India’s inflation rate has varied between 4% and 6% in recent years[1]. This steady rise can have an impact on long-term financial planning, especially for goals like education and healthcare.
Understanding Child Plans
Child plans are financial investments that assist parents save and invest for their children's future. These plans typically include a combination of insurance and investment components, ensuring that funds are available for significant milestones like higher education or marriage[3].
Features of Child Plans:
Life Cover: Provides financial protection for the child in case of the policyholder’s demise[3].
Savings and Growth: Helps build a corpus over time through investments in equity, debt, or hybrid funds.
Flexible Payouts: Allows partial withdrawals at key milestones.
Tax Benefits: Offers tax exemptions under Section 80C and Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act[4].
How Inflation Impacts Child Plans
1. Erosion of Purchasing Power
Parents are finding it difficult to build a successful financial base due to inflation. Even if you accumulate a large corpus, inflation reduces its real value[5]. A sum assured of ₹20 lakh today may only cover expenses worth ₹10–12 lakh in the future due to the declining value of money.
2. Rising Education Costs
India's education inflation rose by 1% in May 2021, to 4.09% in 2022. This shows how necessary it is for you as a parent to secure funds for your child's higher education. You need to ensure that the corpus you build is enough to meet your child's educational expense keeping the inflation rate in mind[5].
3. Healthcare Costs
Healthcare costs are heavily impacted by inflation too. In India, healthcare expenses are rising at the rate of 14%, in case of illnesses, your savings could take a big hit [5].
Steps to Protect Your Child’s Future Against Inflation
Start Early The earlier you begin, the more time you have to grow your money giving you chances to take bigger risks and earn more money in the long run. If parents start earning and saving for their children early, they’ll be better prepared to handle future expenses[6]. Prioritize Financial GoalsPlan and prioritize your child’s goals and ensure each one of them is covered separately to increase the likelihood of achieving them. Consider separate term plans for vital goals[6]. Select a Premium Waiver Plan Choose a child plan that includes a premium waiver in case of unforeseen circumstances, ensuring your child’s education continues without financial strain[6]. Include Partial Withdrawal PlansPrepare for emergencies by including partial withdrawal options in your child’s education fund, providing easy access to money when needed urgently[6].
How to Select the Right Child Plan
1. Check Past Performance
While past performance doesn’t guarantee future results, it indicates how the plan has performed during inflationary periods.
2. Look for Flexibility
Choose plans that offer flexible payout options or allow partial withdrawals. This ensures you can access funds when needed without compromising the overall goal.
3. Evaluate Additional Benefits
Riders like critical illness coverage, waiver of premium, or accidental death benefits can add extra layers of security to your plan.
4. Account for Inflation in Sum AssuredOpt for plans that offer increasing sum assured or inflation-adjusted payouts to ensure the corpus keeps pace with rising costs over time.
Conclusion
Inflation is an unavoidable reality, but its impact on child plans can be mitigated through informed planning and strategic investments. Start early, choose a plan with inflation-beating potential, and review your progress periodically. By doing so, you can ensure your child’s future is financially secure and unaffected by rising costs.Inflation impacts child plans, but starting early, choosing plans with inflation-beating potential, and periodic reviews can effectively secure your child’s future, ensuring rising costs don’t compromise their financial security.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
1. What is the impact of inflation on child plans?
Inflation reduces money's purchasing power, so without properly accounting for it, a child plan's corpus may fall short of future expenses, especially with rising education and healthcare costs over time.2. How can I ensure my child's plan keeps up with inflation?
To ensure your child plan keeps up with inflation, you can:
Opt for plans with equity or hybrid fund investments for higher returns.
Periodically increase the sum assured.
Regularly examine and adjust your plan to align with inflation trends.
3. Are child plans a good way to save for my child’s education?
Yes, child plans are effective as they combine insurance with investment, ensuring financial security and wealth accumulation.[1]https://www.thehindu.com/business/Economy/rbi-raises-inflation-outlook-to-48-for-fy25/article68953928.ece#:~:text=The%20Reserve%20Bank%20of%20India,%25%20and%20Q2%20at%204%25.[2] https://www.investopedia.com/terms/i/inflation.asp[3]  https://www.5paisa.com/stock-market-guide/insurance/what-is-child-insurance-plan [4] https://cleartax.in/s/term-insurance-tax-benefits[5]https://www.livemint.com/money/personal-finance/high-inflation-these-3-sectors-can-affect-your-child-s-future-151670321784198.html[6]https://groww.in/blog/planning-a-childs-futureDisclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Inflation and Child Insurance Plans: What Must You Do?
If you look at the past five years (2019-2024), the inflation retail rate has not fallen below 4%[1]. As a parent, planning for your child's future is one of the most important financial decisions to make. Inflation significantly impacts this process. In this blog, we’ll explore the impact of inflation on child plans, the steps you can take to safeguard your investments, and practical strategies to ensure your child’s future is financially secure.
What is Inflation and Why Does it Matter?
Inflation is the rate at which the prices of goods and services rise over time, lowering the purchasing buying power of money[2]. India’s inflation rate has varied between 4% and 6% in the recent years[1]. This steady rise can have an impact on long-term financial planning, especially for goals like education and healthcare.
Understanding Child Insurance Plans
Child insurance plans are financial investmentstype of life child insurance plans that assist parents to save and invest for their children's future. These plans typically include a combination of insurance and investment components, ensuring that funds are available for significant milestones like higher education or marriage[3]. For instance you could opt for SIPs, if you have a girl child then SSY (Sukanya Samruddhi Yojana), ULIPs or Mutual Funds. After a thorough research you will be able to udnderstand how much money you can shell out each month and invest accordingly.
Features of Child Insurance Plans:
Life Cover: Provides financial protection for the child. The extent of coverage is proportionate to the premium you payin case of the policyholder’s demise[3].
Savings and Growth: Helps build a corpus over time through investments in equity, debt, or hybrid funds. Flexible Payouts: Allows partial withdrawals at key milestones subject to policy terms & conditions. Do note that partial withdrawals are only applicable in case of ULIPs. Tax Benefits: Offers tax exemptions under Section 80C (in case of old tax regime) and Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act (subject to certain conditions).[4].
How Inflation Impacts Child Insurance Plans
1. Erosion of Purchasing Power

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
1. What is the impact of inflation on child plans?

Yes, child plans are effective as they combine insurance with investment, ensuring financial security and wealth accumulation.
